title: The finite mean is additive

source.citation: Lloyd N. Trefethen, Spectral Methods in MATLAB, SIAM, 2000, Ch. 3, pp. 17-28.
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: For finite signals u and v, the finite arithmetic mean of their pointwise sum equals the sum of their finite means.
assumptions: u and v are real-valued signals

declarations.name: finiteDC
declarations.statement: def finiteDC {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (u : ι → Real) : Real := ∑ i ∈ s, u i

declarations.name: finiteMean
declarations.statement: def finiteMean {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (u : ι → Real) : Real := finiteDC s u / (s.card : Real)

declarations.name: finiteMean_add
declarations.statement: theorem finiteMean_add {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (u v : ι → Real) : finiteMean s (fun i => u i + v i) = finiteMean s u + finiteMean s v

tasks.input: Translate the finite-index spectral/DC identity `finiteMean_add` into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem finiteMean_add {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (u v : ι → Real) : finiteMean s (fun i => u i + v i) = finiteMean s u + finiteMean s v
Unfold finiteMean, use finiteDC_add, and normalize division algebra.

tasks.input: Identify the main finite-sum or mean-centering lemma needed for the proof.
